navigator.bluetooth.requestDevice.summary: Prompts the user to select a nearby BLE peripheral matching the provided filters. Must be called from a user activation (click/tap). Returns a BluetoothDevice; rejects with NotFoundError if the user cancels or no device matches.
caveats: Requires a user gesture — calling from a timer or promise microtask throws SecurityError.
Requires a secure context (HTTPS).
Declare every service you plan to access in filters or optionalServices — anything else is blocked with SecurityError.
Cannot combine acceptAllDevices:true with filters.
On WebBLE, the chooser UI is driven by the Safari Web Extension; styling cannot be customised from the page.

navigator.bluetooth.getAvailability.summary: Reports whether the browser has a usable Bluetooth radio and (on WebBLE) whether the extension is reachable on the current origin. Returns Promise<boolean>.
caveats: On WebBLE, false may mean the extension is installed but disabled or not granted permission on this origin — not necessarily that the radio is off.
Always check before calling requestDevice() to show a helpful message rather than a generic rejection.

BluetoothDevice.gatt.connect.summary: Opens a GATT connection to the peripheral. Returns the connected BluetoothRemoteGATTServer; rejects with NetworkError if the peripheral is out of range or refuses the connection.
caveats: After every reconnect, all previously obtained service / characteristic / descriptor handles are invalidated — re-fetch them.
Serialize concurrent connect() calls from different code paths.
Listen for 'gattserverdisconnected' on the BluetoothDevice to react to drops.

BluetoothDevice.gatt.disconnect.summary: Tears down the GATT connection. Fires gattserverdisconnected synchronously on the device.
caveats: Does not unpair the device — only closes the active GATT link.
Subsequent operations on stale handles fail with NetworkError.

BluetoothRemoteGATTServer.getPrimaryServ.summary: Returns a primary GATT service advertised by the peripheral. Accepts a 16-bit alias ('heart_rate'), 128-bit UUID string, or numeric service ID.
caveats: Rejects with NotFoundError if the service is absent or wasn't listed in filters/optionalServices at requestDevice() time.
UUID name aliases must match the Bluetooth SIG assigned-numbers table exactly (lowercase underscore form).

BluetoothRemoteGATTService.getCharacteri.summary: Returns a single GATT characteristic of this service by 16-bit alias, 128-bit UUID string, or numeric ID.
caveats: Rejects with NotFoundError when the characteristic is absent from the peripheral's firmware — enumerate with service.getCharacteristics() to discover what is actually exposed.
Handles are invalidated by any reconnect.

BluetoothRemoteGATTCharacteristic.readVa.summary: Performs a GATT read and returns a DataView over the raw bytes. Also updates characteristic.value.
caveats: Only valid when char.properties.read is true — otherwise rejects with NotSupportedError.
Returns a DataView; use getUint8/getUint16(offset, littleEndian)/getFloat32 to decode. Check endianness against the profile spec.

BluetoothRemoteGATTCharacteristic.writeV.summary: Writes the given BufferSource to the characteristic. Legacy API — prefer writeValueWithResponse() / writeValueWithoutResponse() when you need explicit control.
caveats: Only valid when char.properties.write or writeWithoutResponse is true.
Values longer than the negotiated ATT MTU are fragmented automatically but may still be rejected by constrained peripherals.

BluetoothRemoteGATTCharacteristic.startN.summary: Subscribes to value-change notifications. Each fire dispatches a 'characteristicvaluechanged' event with the new bytes at event.target.value.
caveats: Requires char.properties.notify or char.properties.indicate.
Call stopNotifications() to unsubscribe; on disconnect the subscription is torn down automatically and must be re-established after reconnect.
Safari foreground only unless a window.webbleIOS.backgroundSync registration keeps the channel alive.
